message:
So there I was, weather alert blaring on my phone during that gnarly thunderstorm last month—and of course, my battery was at 2%. I'm frantically trying to read the alert while also looking for my flashlight (which was, naturally, in a drawer I couldn't find in the dark). That's when it hit me: I'd never actually sat down and figured out what I'd do if my phone went dead *during* an emergency.

Turned out I wasn't alone—talked to a neighbor the next day who said the same thing happened to her. We both realized we were kind of banking on our phones to be our emergency command center, but what if they weren't?

Now I keep a little printed card with important numbers and a basic checklist of what to do in different storms. Sounds old-school, but honestly? It's weirdly comforting to have something that doesn't need a battery. Plus a dedicated weather radio that runs on batteries I actually replace.

Have you ever thought about what you'd do if your phone died right when you needed it most?
